title: Determination of the lipid composition of the GPI anchor
author: Auxiliadora Aguilera-Romero †
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bvban2ie

steps.title: Transform the yeast strain with a...
steps.instruction: Transform the yeast strain with a centromeric plasmid expressing GFP-tagged Gas1p under control of its own promoter (pRS416-GAS1-GFP).

steps.title: Pick up a colony and streak...
steps.instruction: Pick up a colony and streak it on a SD agar plate with appropriate nutritional supplements (SC-URA) and incubate them at 24 °C for 2-3 days.

steps.title: Inoculate transformed yeast cells in 3ml...
steps.instruction: Inoculate transformed yeast cells in 3ml of SC-URA in a 12 ml sterile tube and growth them to the early-to-mid logarithmic phase at 24 °C with shaking at 250 rpm.

steps.title: Wash PVDF membrane strips slowly avoiding...
steps.instruction: Wash PVDF membrane strips slowly avoiding touching the membrane with 1ml of methanol (MS quality) four times.

steps.title: Wash PVDF strips with 1ml of water (MS quality)
steps.instruction: Wash PVDF strips with 1ml of water (MS quality).

steps.title: Incubate with a filtered mixture of 500 μl of 0
steps.instruction: Incubate with a filtered mixture of 500 μl of 0.3 M NaOAc pH 4.0 buffer and 500 μl of freshly dissolved 1 M NaNO 2 for 3 h at 37 °C.

steps.title: Remove the solution and wash the...
steps.instruction: Remove the solution and wash the PVDF membrane strips with 1 ml of water MS-LC grade four times.

steps.title: Extract the GPI-lipids from the PVDF...
steps.instruction: Extract the GPI-lipids from the PVDF strip with 75 μl of negative mode solvent (Chloroform/Methanol (1:2) + 5 mM Ammonium acetate).

steps.title: Transfer the sample into a 96...
steps.instruction: Transfer the sample into a 96 well plate and seal with an Easy Pierce 20 µm Foil to avoid evaporation of the sample.

steps.title: Infuse the sample on a TSQ...
steps.instruction: Infuse the sample on a TSQ Vantage using a Triversa Nanomate equipped with a HD_A chip with a gas pressure of 30 psi and a spray voltage of 1.2 kV.

steps.title: Operate the TSQ Vantage with a...
steps.instruction: Operate the TSQ Vantage with a spray voltage of 3 kV in negative-ion mode. The capillary temperature is set to 190 °C.

steps.title: Use Multiple Reaction Monitoring (MRM) to...
steps.instruction: Use Multiple Reaction Monitoring (MRM) to obtain the signal intensity for each lipid molecular species (Fragmentation is performed with a collision energy optimized for each lipid class)

steps.title: Read 3 times the MRM list...
steps.instruction: Read 3 times the MRM list (Table 1) on the TSQ Vantage to obtain three technical replicates for each biological replicate of the strain (It is advisable, to include at least, 3 biological replicates per strain) The MRM measurements are done with a width set to 1.0 in the Q3.

steps.title: Calculate the signal intensity for each...
steps.instruction: Calculate the signal intensity for each parent ion adding the intensities obtained in a range of ± 0.5 the m/z of the production. This can be done manually with Thermo Xcalibur software.

steps.title: Finally, calculate the proportional signal for...
steps.instruction: Finally, calculate the proportional signal for each lipid species compared to the sum of all the signals.

steps.title: Dilute yeast cells into 800 ml...
steps.instruction: Dilute yeast cells into 800 ml of YPD medium and growth them to mid-log phase overnight at 30°C with shaking at 250 rpm. YPD medium is required for the correct expression of Gas1-GFP at the cell surface.

steps.title: On the next day, harvest 600...
steps.instruction: On the next day, harvest 600 x 10 7 cells by centrifugation at 3000 x g for 5 min, discard the supernatant, resuspend in 12 ml of TNE precooled at 4°C and distribute aliquots of 1 ml into 2 ml screw-cap tubes (12 tubes).

steps.title: Centrifuge aliquots at 13,000 x g...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge aliquots at 13,000 x g for 1 min, discard supernatant and freeze the pellet at -80 °C.

steps.title: Break cells using Fast Prep (3...
steps.instruction: Break cells using Fast Prep (3 pulses of 30 s at 5 m/s).

steps.title: Remove glass beads and cell debris...
steps.instruction: Remove glass beads and cell debris by centrifugation at 1000 x g for 10 min at 4 °C.

steps.title: Transfer 1 ml of supernatant into a 1
steps.instruction: Transfer 1 ml of supernatant into a 1.5 ml ultraclear tube (UC).

steps.title: Centrifuge at 17,000 x g for...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 17,000 x g for 20 min at 4 °C.

steps.title: Resuspend and combine all the pellets into a single 1
steps.instruction: Resuspend and combine all the pellets into a single 1.5 ml UC tube using 1 ml of TNE.

steps.title: Add 250µl of TNE-D 5 %...
steps.instruction: Add 250µl of TNE-D 5 % to obtain a solution with a final concentration of 1% digitonin. Mix with end-over-end rotation for 1 h at 4 °C.

steps.title: Centrifuge the suspension at 17,000 x...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the suspension at 17,000 x g for 20 min at 4 °C to remove insoluble material.

steps.title: Transfer the supernatant to an UC...
steps.instruction: Transfer the supernatant to an UC tube with 100 µl GFP-Trap beads at (30 % slurry) and incubate overnight at 4 °C.

steps.title: Next day, centrifuge at 5,000 x...
steps.instruction: Next day, centrifuge at 5,000 x g for 30 s at 4 °C and remove supernatant.

steps.title: Resuspend the beads pellet with 500...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the beads pellet with 500 µl of TNE-D 1 % and transfer to a new UC tube.

steps.title: Wash the original UC tube with...
steps.instruction: Wash the original UC tube with 500 µl of TNE-D 1 % and combine the beads suspension with the first one.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 5,000 x g for...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 5,000 x g for 30 s at 4 °C and remove the supernatant.

steps.title: Add 750 µl TNE-D 0
steps.instruction: Add 750 µl TNE-D 0.2 %. Repeat step 19 and 20 twice more.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 5,000 x g for...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 5,000 x g for 30 s at 4 °C and remove the supernatant. Centrifuge again to remove the remaining liquid and dry the beads using a white micropipette tip.

steps.title: Add 40 µl of SB 2x
steps.instruction: Add 40 µl of SB 2x.

steps.title: Heat the samples at 95 °C for 10 min
steps.instruction: Heat the samples at 95 °C for 10 min.

steps.title: Vortex
steps.instruction: Vortex. Centrifuge maximum speed for 5 min at room temperature.

steps.title: Load the supernatant onto a 7
steps.instruction: Load the supernatant onto a 7.5 % acrylamide gel, separate the proteins by SDS-PAGE gel electrophoresis and transfer to a PVDF membrane previously activated by methanol (Wet the PVDF membrane in 100 % methanol for a few seconds and then equilibrate the membrane in transfer buffer for a few minutes until it sinks).

steps.title: After transfer, stain the PVDF membrane...
steps.instruction: After transfer, stain the PVDF membrane with Amido Black Staining Solution 2x for 1min.

steps.title: Wash five times with the destaining solution for 2 min
steps.instruction: Wash five times with the destaining solution for 2 min.

steps.title: Cut out Gas1-GFP band with a...
steps.instruction: Cut out Gas1-GFP band with a new razor blade and transfer to an amber glass tube or screw-cap Eppendorf tube.

steps.title: Add 300 µl of glass beads and 1
steps.instruction: Add 300 µl of glass beads and 1.5 ml of TNE to each cell pellet.

steps.title: Dry and transfer the membrane to an amber tube
steps.instruction: Dry and transfer the membrane to an amber tube. Flush the samples with N 2 and store at -80 °C.
